title: FIT — NAICS 221122 Electric Power Distribution (DSO view, hardware tier)

sections.title: Step 09 — Technology-Level Feature–Market Fit
sections.content:
### 09a — Per-market Kano + 9-dim ODI fit (6 technology-scope features)

Scale: 1 = no contribution, 5 = moderate, 10 = best-known real-world. `overall_fit` = arithmetic mean.

| # | Feature | Kano | Time | Cost | Safety | Reliab. | Skill | Stress | Pain | Confid. | Delight | Overall | Primary drivers |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Containerized Li-ion BESS Integration | must_be | 4 | 5 | 6 | 8 | 3 | 5 | 4 | 7 | 2 | **4.89** | Reliability, Confidence — prefab container is the category baseline for DSO deployment speed and LFP cycle life |
| F2 | Bidirectional Power Conversion System | must_be | 6 | 5 | 5 | 8 | 3 | 6 | 4 | 8 | 3 | **5.33** | Reliability, Confidence — 4-quadrant PCS is table-stakes for FCR-grade sub-200 ms dispatch (need #9) |
| F3 | Medium-Voltage Grid Coupling | must_be | 5 | 4 | 7 | 8 | 4 | 5 | 3 | 8 | 2 | **5.11** | Safety, Reliability, Confidence — protection relays + NA-protection are the DSO acceptance gate |
| F4 | Energy Management System (EMS) | one_dimensional | 8 | 7 | 4 | 8 | 6 | 7 | 7 | 7 | 6 | **6.67** | Time, Reliability, Pain — dispatch accuracy (#9), operator interventions (#13), availability (#8) scale linearly with EMS quality |
| F5 | Grid-Code Certification Delivery | must_be | 9 | 6 | 8 | 7 | 4 | 8 | 7 | 9 | 4 | **6.89** | Time, Confidence, Safety — highest-opportunity need in market (#4, O=15); absence = no energization |
| F8 | Civil Works & Site Preparation | must_be | 5 | 4 | 6 | 6 | 4 | 4 | 5 | 5 | 2 | **4.56** | Safety, Time — Baugenehmigung + Feuerwehr clearance (#6) carry the rework risk |

**Rationale (consolidated):** All six are must_be — any BESS EPC must deliver them to the DSO (commodity FP grounding). EMS is one_dimensional because DSO dispatch quality (response error, availability, manual interventions) scales continuously with EMS sophistication. No feature is attractive in this market: DSOs treat BESS as infrastructure, not a delighter. No reverse features — DSO regulatory regime does not forbid any of these capabilities.

### 09b — Validation checks

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Cross-market Kano consistency | PASS — must_be in DSO aligns with must_be in adjacent 221118/221114/221121 utility markets |
| safety ≥7 & confidence ≤3 | none flagged (F3 safety=7, confidence=8; F5 safety=8, confidence=9) |
| skill ≥7 & stress ≤3 | none flagged |
| Overall_fit arithmetic | recomputed deterministically — all match to 2 dp |
| No 10s-across-all | PASS (max dim = 9, F5 time/confidence) |
| Feature names (1.pdf rules) | PASS — 2–6 words, spec-visible, no brand/outcome/superlative |

### 09c — Neo4j writes (preview)

```
MERGE (f:Feature {name, run_id})-[:kano_for_market {
  kano_class, fit_score_time..delight, overall_fit,
  feature_scope:"technology", rationale
}]->(m:Market {naics_code:"221122", run_id})
```

---

sections.title: Step 10 — Product-Related Needs (Burleson Product Jobs)
sections.content:
Litmus: would this need disappear if a different technology fulfilled the same commodity FP (e.g., copper reinforcement A4, STATCOM A2, VPP A3)? YES → product-related.

### 10a — Generated needs (13 entries, one-to-many from 9 constraints)

| # | Statement (error-first) | Source constraint | Affected markets (221122 only unless noted) | I | S | Litmus |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PR1 | Minimize the likelihood of grid-code certification (VDE-AR-N 4110 Type B / FGW TR8) slipping the DSO project critical path by >3 months | Grid Code Certification Lead Time | 221122 | 9 | 3 | YES — copper/STATCOM use different or no plant-cert path |
| PR2 | Minimize the time to achieve DSO acceptance after commissioning for a containerized LFP plant at a contested MV feeder | Grid Code Certification Lead Time | 221122 | 8 | 4 | YES — A4 copper has no type-B plant cert; VPP has no physical asset |
| PR3 | Minimize the likelihood of thermal-runaway propagation across adjacent modules or containers during a single-cell fault | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | 221122 | 10 | 6 | YES — STATCOM/copper/VPP have no Li-ion chemistry |
| PR4 | Minimize the likelihood of Feuerwehr or VdS rejection of container siting due to insufficient clearance or hydrant access | Fire-Protection Clearance | 221122 | 9 | 5 | YES — fire clearance is specific to energy-storage container footprints |
| PR5 | Minimize the likelihood of Baugenehmigung denial driven by 40–80 m²/MWh footprint in DSO substation yard lots | Land Footprint and Siting | 221122 | 8 | 5 | YES — copper feeder upgrade and VPP have no container footprint; STATCOM footprint is smaller |
| PR6 | Minimize the likelihood of round-trip efficiency falling below 85% AC-AC after 5 years of contracted DSO cycling | Round-Trip Energy Losses | 221122 | 7 | 5 | YES — STATCOM/copper have no RTE concept; VPP loss profile differs |
| PR7 | Minimize the likelihood of capacity fade >3% per year beyond warranty curve going undetected for >30 days | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard (proxy: LFP calendar ageing) | 221122 | 8 | 4 | YES — Li-ion specific degradation mode |
| PR8 | Minimize the likelihood of simultaneous unavailability during a redispatch event because SoC is parked too high or too low to serve the DSO command | Round-Trip Energy Losses (SoC operating-window compression) | 221122 | 9 | 4 | YES — SoC-bound availability is Li-ion-specific; copper/STATCOM always available |
| PR9 | Minimize the likelihood of HVAC-driven efficiency collapse at ambient extremes below −20 °C or above +50 °C on rural DSO sites | Ambient Temperature Envelope | 221122 | 6 | 5 | YES — copper/STATCOM are ambient-agnostic |
| PR10 | Minimize the time to restore full dispatch capability after a single PCS or battery-module fault (target <8 h) | MV Grid Connection Capacity (PCS bottleneck) + Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | 221122 | 8 | 5 | YES — copper has no single-point PCS/module fault mode |
| PR11 | Minimize the likelihood of DSO tender-price variance exceeding ±10% across lots of similar scope driven by LFP cell supply volatility (FX, tariff, CRMA) | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | 221122 | 7 | 4 | YES — copper/STATCOM supply chain is DE/EU-diverse; VPP has no hardware |
| PR12 | Minimize the likelihood that the storage business case closes below €250/kWh installed or collapses above €400/kWh in DSO framework contracts | CAPEX Viability Threshold | 221122 | 8 | 5 | YES — STATCOM capex is decoupled from €/kWh; copper capex is €/km |
| PR13 | Minimize the likelihood of end-of-life recycling non-compliance (EU Battery Regulation, CRMA) imposing retroactive cost on the DSO as asset owner | Battery Supply Chain Concentration (regulatory tail) | 221122 | 7 | 4 | YES — EU Battery Reg applies only to battery chemistries |

Unused constraints map: **MV Grid Connection Capacity** → core of CFJ for DSO, already a market need (#1, #2, #12) — *not* product-related because copper reinforcement A4 also requires MV capacity assessment. Retained implicit in PR10.

### 10b — Validation

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Litmus test (would disappear under A2/A3/A4) | PASS for all 13 |
| Source-constraint traceability | PASS — every `source_constraint` exists in Step 01 constraints list |
| Affected-market plausibility | PASS — DSO-only market scope stated |
| Completeness (every constraint → ≥1 need) | PASS — 9/9 covered (MV-capacity covered via PR10 blend) |
| Low-I / high-S near-dropouts | none (all I ≥ 6, all S ≤ 6) |
| "Minimize/Increase" error-first phrasing | PASS — all "Minimize the likelihood/time" |
| Deduplication vs Step 06 market needs | PASS — none of 13 duplicate JTBD market-level needs |

### 10c — Neo4j writes (preview)

```
MERGE (es:ErrorStatement {statement[:120], run_id}) SET es.product_related=true, es.source_constraint, es.importance, es.satisfaction_current
MERGE (c:Constraint {name, run_id})-[:generates_need]->(es)
MERGE (npg:NeutralProductGroup {name:"Large-Scale Battery Energy Storage System EPC"})-[:has_product_need]->(es)
MERGE (es)-[:applies_to_market]->(m:Market {naics_code:"221122", run_id})
```

---

sections.title: Step 11 — Vendor Feature–Market Fit (Mein Solar GmbH)
sections.content:
**Best incumbent in this market:** Fluence Gridstack/Smartstack (~30% DE DSO share, tier-1 Kupferzell reference). Where Fluence is not the leader on a specific feature (e.g., PV integration, single-source contract for small-scale DSO framework), next-best incumbent is noted.

### 11a — Vendor vs best-incumbent comparison

Vendor-scope features (6, 7, 9) plus tech-scope features where Mein Solar's *implementation* materially differs from the generic baseline.

| Feature | Scope | Kano (DSO) | Vendor (Mein Solar) | Best incumbent | Incumbent tech | Advantage | Rationale (spec-anchored) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Co-located PV+BESS Hybrid Design | vendor | attractive | **8** | 6 | Fluence Gridstack + third-party PV EPC | **superior** | Mein Solar delivers PV+BESS under one EPC contract, one VDE-AR-N 4110 cert, one EMS. Fluence treats PV as external input; requires second EPC to wire PV. Unlocks DG-saturated feeder (S3) Sc5 curtailment recovery. |
| EV-Charging Buffer Configuration | vendor | indifferent | 6 | 5 | ads-tec Energy ChargeBox | **parity** | DSO-owned Netzbooster is rarely a HPC-buffer use case; capability exists but market barely uses it. Scored 6/5 on raw capability but Kano = indifferent in DSO context. |
| Single-Source EPC Contract Vehicle | vendor | one_dimensional | **7** | 5 | Fluence turnkey | **superior** | Mein Solar wraps battery + PCS + MV + civil + cert + O&M in one warranty. Fluence turnkey covers its own stack but DSO still contracts civil + MV separately in DE framework tenders. Reduces DSO interface count to ≤1 (need #5). |
| Containerized Li-ion BESS Integration | tech | must_be | 6 | **9** | Fluence Gridstack (4th-gen modular) | **inferior** | Fluence ships standardized Gridstack containers at scale with 10-yr performance guarantee; Mein Solar re-integrates third-party containers (SolarEast, Sungrow). Reliability at ≥98% availability (#8) favors tier-1 OEM product. |
| Bidirectional Power Conversion System | tech | must_be | 6 | **8** | Sungrow PowerTitan PCS / SMA Sunny Central Storage | **inferior** | Tier-1 PCS OEMs ship <100 ms FCR-qualified inverters as native product. Mein Solar integrates SMA/Sungrow PCS — parity at spec level, slightly behind on proprietary optimization. |
| Medium-Voltage Grid Coupling | tech | must_be | **8** | 7 | Siemens Grid Integration / Hitachi Energy | **parity** | Mein Solar's incumbent PV-EPC practice delivers VDE-AR-N 4110 MV scope to DSO-acceptance standard. Hitachi/Siemens equal or slightly better on IEC 61850 utility interop; Mein Solar stronger on DE-specific DSO process fluency. |
| Energy Management System (EMS) | tech | one_dimensional | 5 | **8** | Fluence Mosaic / Tesla Autobidder / Wärtsilä GEMS | **inferior** | Closed-loop optimization (forecast + SoC + degradation + market) is tier-1 OEMs' strongest moat. Mein Solar typically integrates third-party EMS (Entrix, Next Kraftwerke) rather than owning the stack. Dispatch accuracy (#9) and availability (#8) depend on EMS sophistication. |
| Grid-Code Certification Delivery | tech | must_be | **9** | 7 | Fluence DE team | **superior** | Mein Solar's PV-EPC history = hundreds of VDE-AR-N 4105/4110 certifications already delivered; native FGW TR8 process knowledge. Tier-1 OEMs rely on in-country partners for German cert; schedule risk higher. Addresses need #4 (O=15). |
| Civil Works & Site Preparation | tech | must_be | **7** | 6 | Goldbeck Solar / site-specific subcontractors | **parity** | Mein Solar self-performs civil via PV-EPC crew; tier-1 OEMs subcontract. Reduces Baugenehmigung friction (need #6) marginally; gap is within 1 point. |

### 11b — Objectivity review (bias audit)

| Metric | Value | Check |
|---|---|---|
| Total comparisons | 9 | — |
| superior | 3 (33%) | within objective range; not >70% |
| parity | 3 (33%) | — |
| inferior | 3 (33%) | not >70%; balanced |
| Vendor_score ≥9 frequency | 1/9 (11%) | acceptable (F5 cert delivery — defensible) |
| Incumbent_score ≤3 frequency | 0/9 | no straw-man |
| Rationale spec-specificity | 9/9 reference concrete capability | PASS |
| Kano appropriateness | must_be pairs both ≥5 ✅; attractive F6 has vendor>incumbent ✅ | PASS |
| Advantage arithmetic | |Δ|≤1 = parity (3); >1 vendor = superior (3); >1 incumbent = inferior (3) | PASS |

**Net positioning for DSO market:** Mein Solar is a **mid-tier integrator** in 221122. Superior on the EPC methodology layer (PV+BESS hybrid, single-source contract, VDE-AR-N 4110 cert). Inferior on the three deep-OEM capabilities (container product, PCS, EMS) where Fluence / Tesla / Sungrow have proprietary R&D that an integrator cannot match. Best-fit DSO opportunity: framework contracts where single-source EPC + fast DE cert > proprietary product edge (smaller Stadtwerke DSOs, PV+BESS hybrid deployments); worst-fit: large Netzbetreiber tenders where tier-1 product performance SLAs dominate (Bayernwerk, Netze BW major framework lots).

### 11c — Neo4j writes (preview)

```
MERGE (f:Feature {name, run_id}) SET f.feature_scope="vendor", f.description
MERGE (p:Product {name:"EPC für Großbatteriespeicher", company_name:"Mein Solar GmbH", run_id})
MERGE (p)-[:has_feature]->(f)
MERGE (f)-[:kano_for_market {kano_class, vendor_score, best_incumbent_score, best_incumbent_technology, advantage, feature_scope:"vendor", rationale}]->(m:Market {naics_code:"221122", run_id})
```

---

rows.Statement (error-first): Minimize the likelihood of grid-code certification (VDE-AR-N 4110 Type B / FGW TR8) slipping the DSO project critical path by >3 months

rows.Statement (error-first): Minimize the time to achieve DSO acceptance after commissioning for a containerized LFP plant at a contested MV feeder
rows.Litmus: YES — A4 copper has no type-B plant cert; VPP has no physical asset

rows.Statement (error-first): Minimize the likelihood of thermal-runaway propagation across adjacent modules or containers during a single-cell fault

rows.Statement (error-first): Minimize the likelihood of Feuerwehr or VdS rejection of container siting due to insufficient clearance or hydrant access
rows.Litmus: YES — fire clearance is specific to energy-storage container footprints

rows.Statement (error-first): Minimize the likelihood of Baugenehmigung denial driven by 40–80 m²/MWh footprint in DSO substation yard lots
rows.Litmus: YES — copper feeder upgrade and VPP have no container footprint; STATCOM footprint is smaller

rows.Statement (error-first): Minimize the likelihood of round-trip efficiency falling below 85% AC-AC after 5 years of contracted DSO cycling
rows.Litmus: YES — STATCOM/copper have no RTE concept; VPP loss profile differs

rows.Statement (error-first): Minimize the likelihood of capacity fade >3% per year beyond warranty curve going undetected for >30 days
rows.Source constraint: Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard (proxy: LFP calendar ageing)

rows.Statement (error-first): Minimize the likelihood of simultaneous unavailability during a redispatch event because SoC is parked too high or too low to serve the DSO command

rows.Statement (error-first): Minimize the likelihood of HVAC-driven efficiency collapse at ambient extremes below −20 °C or above +50 °C on rural DSO sites

rows.Litmus: YES — copper has no single-point PCS/module fault mode

rows.Statement (error-first): Minimize the likelihood of DSO tender-price variance exceeding ±10% across lots of similar scope driven by LFP cell supply volatility (FX, tariff, CRMA)
rows.Litmus: YES — copper/STATCOM supply chain is DE/EU-diverse; VPP has no hardware

rows.Statement (error-first): Minimize the likelihood that the storage business case closes below €250/kWh installed or collapses above €400/kWh in DSO framework contracts

rows.Statement (error-first): Minimize the likelihood of end-of-life recycling non-compliance (EU Battery Regulation, CRMA) imposing retroactive cost on the DSO as asset owner

rows.Rationale (spec-anchored): Mein Solar delivers PV+BESS under one EPC contract, one VDE-AR-N 4110 cert, one EMS. Fluence treats PV as external input; requires second EPC to wire PV. Unlocks DG-saturated feeder (S3) Sc5 curtailment recovery.
DSO-owned Netzbooster is rarely a HPC-buffer use case; capability exists but market barely uses it. Scored 6/5 on raw capability but Kano = indifferent in DSO context.

Mein Solar wraps battery + PCS + MV + civil + cert + O&M in one warranty. Fluence turnkey covers its own stack but DSO still contracts civil + MV separately in DE framework tenders. Reduces DSO interface count to ≤1 (need #5).

Fluence ships standardized Gridstack containers at scale with 10-yr performance guarantee; Mein Solar re-integrates third-party containers (SolarEast, Sungrow). Reliability at ≥98% availability (#8) favors tier-1 OEM product.

rows.Incumbent tech: Sungrow PowerTitan PCS / SMA Sunny Central Storage
rows.Rationale (spec-anchored): Tier-1 PCS OEMs ship <100 ms FCR-qualified inverters as native product. Mein Solar integrates SMA/Sungrow PCS — parity at spec level, slightly behind on proprietary optimization.

Mein Solar's incumbent PV-EPC practice delivers VDE-AR-N 4110 MV scope to DSO-acceptance standard. Hitachi/Siemens equal or slightly better on IEC 61850 utility interop; Mein Solar stronger on DE-specific DSO process fluency.

rows.Incumbent tech: Fluence Mosaic / Tesla Autobidder / Wärtsilä GEMS
rows.Rationale (spec-anchored): Closed-loop optimization (forecast + SoC + degradation + market) is tier-1 OEMs' strongest moat. Mein Solar typically integrates third-party EMS (Entrix, Next Kraftwerke) rather than owning the stack. Dispatch accuracy (#9) and availability (#8) depend on EMS sophistication.

Mein Solar's PV-EPC history = hundreds of VDE-AR-N 4105/4110 certifications already delivered; native FGW TR8 process knowledge. Tier-1 OEMs rely on in-country partners for German cert; schedule risk higher. Addresses need #4 (O=15).
Mein Solar self-performs civil via PV-EPC crew; tier-1 OEMs subcontract. Reduces Baugenehmigung friction (need #6) marginally; gap is within 1 point.

rows.Value: must_be pairs both ≥5 ✅; attractive F6 has vendor>incumbent ✅

entities.technology_class: Large-Scale Battery Energy Storage System EPC
entities.commodity_functional_promise: Deliver operational grid-connected electrical energy storage assets as turnkey capital projects with single-source engineering, procurement, construction, and grid-code certification

step_09_tech_feature_fit.rationale: Prefab container is the category baseline for DSO deployment; table-stakes must_be. Primary drivers: Reliability (8) — LFP cycle life underwrites 10-yr contract; Confidence (7) — DSO accepts containerized product as a known unit. Grounded in need #8 (≥98% availability, ≥6000 cycles).
4-quadrant PCS with <100 ms response is the precondition for FCR-grade service (need #9, <2% dispatch error in 200 ms). Must_be in DSO market. Primary drivers: Reliability (8), Confidence (8).

MV transformer + protection relays + NA-protection under VDE-AR-N 4110 are the DSO acceptance gate. Must_be. Primary drivers: Safety (7) — prevents grid fault propagation; Reliability (8); Confidence (8) — DSO SCADA integration cleanliness (need #16).

EMS sophistication scales linearly with DSO value — better forecasting, better SoC management, fewer manual interventions (need #13), higher availability (#8). Primary drivers: Time (8), Reliability (8), Pain (7). Only one_dimensional feature — more genuinely is better.

Need #4 (cert slip >3 months) has O=15 — highest-opportunity need in this market. Absence = asset cannot energize. Primary drivers: Time (9), Confidence (9), Safety (8). Highest overall_fit of all tech features.
Foundation + trenching + Feuerwehr clearance + Baugenehmigung (need #6). Must_be — cannot deploy without it. Primary drivers: Safety (6), Time (5) — rework cost risk if mis-specced.

step_09_validation.overall_fit_arithmetic_recomputed: PASS — all 6 entries match to 2 dp

step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.feature_description: Deliver integrated PV generator and BESS under a single EPC contract with one grid connection and one certification dossier
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.best_incumbent_technology: Fluence Gridstack (BESS) + third-party PV EPC (split contract)
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.rationale: Mein Solar delivers PV+BESS under one contract, one VDE-AR-N 4110 certification, one EMS. Fluence treats PV as external input, requires second EPC for PV. DSO benefit: unlocks Sc5 renewable-curtailment recovery on DG-saturated feeders (S3).

step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.feature_description: Size BESS as buffer for HPC dispensers on weak MV feeders to avoid grid upgrade
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.rationale: DSO-owned Netzbooster rarely functions as HPC buffer (buffer is operator-side). Capability exists but market does not use it; Kano=indifferent in DSO context even though raw capability gap is ~1 point.

step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.feature_description: Wrap battery, PCS, MV, civil, software, and certification under one contract, one schedule, one warranty
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.best_incumbent_technology: Fluence turnkey (wraps own stack; DSO still contracts civil + MV separately)
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.rationale: Mein Solar wraps the entire asset in one warranty. Fluence turnkey covers its Gridstack but DSO manages civil + MV contractors. Directly addresses need #5 (interface count ≤1) and reduces DSO procurement overhead.

step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.feature_description: Supply containerized LFP storage unit with BMS, HVAC, fire suppression
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.rationale: Fluence ships standardized Gridstack containers at scale with 10-yr performance guarantee backed by in-house product R&D. Mein Solar re-integrates third-party containers (SolarEast, Sungrow). Reliability SLA (≥98% availability, need #8) favors tier-1 OEM product.

step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.feature_description: Integrate 4-quadrant PCS with <100 ms response and reactive-power capability
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.best_incumbent_technology: Sungrow PowerTitan native PCS / SMA Sunny Central Storage
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.rationale: Tier-1 PCS OEMs ship FCR-qualified inverters as native product with proprietary firmware. Mein Solar integrates third-party PCS — parity at spec level but 1–2 points behind on vertical optimization for DSO dispatch accuracy (need #9).

step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.feature_description: Deliver MV transformer, SF6-free RMU switchgear, protection relays, NA-protection under VDE-AR-N 4110
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.rationale: Mein Solar's PV-EPC incumbency delivers VDE-AR-N 4110 MV scope to DSO acceptance with DE-specific process fluency. Hitachi/Siemens are slightly better on IEC 61850 utility interop; Mein Solar stronger on DE DSO workflow. Gap within 1 point = parity.

step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.feature_description: Dispatch charge/discharge with forecasting, SoC management, and degradation-aware cycling
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.best_incumbent_technology: Fluence Mosaic / Tesla Autobidder / Wärtsilä GEMS
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.rationale: Tier-1 EMS (closed-loop forecast + SoC + degradation + market) is the strongest OEM moat. Mein Solar integrates third-party EMS (Entrix, Next Kraftwerke) rather than owning the stack — dispatch accuracy (#9) and availability (#8) depend on third-party.

step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.feature_description: Produce VDE-AR-N 4110 Type B plant certificate per FGW TR8 with DSO acceptance
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.rationale: Mein Solar's PV-EPC history delivered hundreds of VDE-AR-N 4105/4110 certifications already; native FGW TR8 process knowledge. Tier-1 OEMs rely on in-country partners; schedule-slip risk is higher. Directly addresses need #4 (O=15, highest-opportunity in this market).

step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.feature_description: Self-perform foundation, trenching, fencing, lightning protection and coordinate Baugenehmigung
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.rationale: Mein Solar self-performs civil via PV-EPC crew with established Baugenehmigung workflow. Tier-1 OEMs subcontract. Marginal advantage on need #6 (fire-protection/permit rework risk) but within 1-point parity band.

net_positioning.summary: Mid-tier integrator in DSO market. Superior on EPC methodology (PV+BESS hybrid, single-source contract, VDE-AR-N 4110 certification). Inferior on three deep-OEM layers (container product, PCS, EMS).
net_positioning.best_fit_dso_opportunity: Framework contracts with smaller Stadtwerke DSOs; PV+BESS hybrid deployments; projects where DE cert speed and single-source accountability outweigh proprietary product edge.
net_positioning.worst_fit_dso_opportunity: Large Netzbetreiber framework tenders (Bayernwerk, Netze BW) where tier-1 product performance SLAs (Fluence Gridstack, Tesla Megapack, Wärtsilä) dominate scoring.
highest_leverage_needs_served: Sc5 curtailment recovery via PV+BESS hybrid
